context: Checkpoint 2026-04-30T17:46Z after continued atomic slices on Homestead/Studio extensibility. Branch develop currently has one local commit after the last pushed CI-green stack unless this handoff is committed/pushed next. Disk remains constrained but stable at about 9.1GB free on /workspaces/refarm; no cleanup was run because free space is stable and cleanup would discard useful build caches.
current_tasks: Pushed CI-green commit 20847fda `refactor(homestead): share studio shell setup`: added `setupStudioShell(...)` in `packages/homestead/src/sdk/Shell.ts`, exported subpath `@refarm.dev/homestead/sdk/shell`, added TS path mappings, and migrated apps/dev plus apps/me pages to use it while keeping host-specific plugin registration explicit. CI run 25178742220 succeeded.

Pushed CI-green commit 371add1f `refactor(homestead): share surface plugin handles`: added `createHomesteadSurfacePluginHandle(...)` in `packages/homestead/src/sdk/plugin-handle.ts`, migrated Studio stream demo and `/surfaces` diagnostics fixtures to it, and documented it in `packages/homestead/README.md`. CI run 25179485963 succeeded.

Local commit df008f9a `feat(homestead): expose mounted surface capabilities`: Homestead now writes `data-refarm-surface-capabilities` onto mounted surface wrappers and emits `surfaceCapabilities` in `ui:surface_mounted` telemetry. The semantic surface inspector parses capabilities from DOM mounts, and the Studio `/surfaces` ledger shows accepted capabilities in mounted rows. This improves auditability of the capability gate.

Current shared SDK primitives in Homestead: `bootStudioRuntime(...)`, `createStudioPluginHandle(...)`, `createHomesteadSurfacePluginHandle(...)`, and `setupStudioShell(...)`. These should be reused by apps/dev, apps/me, and future apps/social instead of copying runtime/shell/plugin-handle boilerplate.

Latest local validations for df008f9a passed: `npm --prefix packages/homestead test -- Shell.test.ts surface-inspector.test.ts`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run type-check`, `npm --prefix packages/homestead run build`, `npm --prefix apps/dev test -- surface-ledger.test.ts`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run type-check`, `npm --prefix apps/dev run build`, `npm run agent:streaming:studio`, `npm run project:validate --silent`, and `git diff --check`.
blockers: Disk is still 99% used on the host mount with about 9.1GB free. This is stable but should be checked before broad builds. Avoid heavy cleanup unless free space drops near 2-3GB or new large artifacts appear.
Builds can rewrite `validations/sqlite-benchmark/browser/public/sqlite3-worker1.mjs`; if it appears modified, inspect and `git restore` it unless intentionally working on that validation artifact.
Rust target dirs were previously cleaned. Avoid broad Rust commands unless Rust is touched; use scoped commands from AGENTS.md.
Known Vite browser externalization warnings for `node:fs/promises` and `node:path` remain non-blocking for apps/dev and apps/me builds.
Moderate npm audit findings remain known/non-critical: Astro, DOMPurify, PostCSS, uuid transitive chains; 0 high/critical in recent pre-push/CI checks.
next_actions: First command after compaction: `git status --branch --short && df -h /workspaces/refarm && git log --oneline -12`.
Commit this handoff update, then push develop and watch CI. The local code commit needing remote confirmation is df008f9a, plus this handoff checkpoint if committed.

Next best atomic slice: add a real executable JS example for a manifest-declared Homestead stream surface under `examples/` that consumes `createHomesteadSurfacePluginHandle(...)` patterns in docs/tests, without introducing host-specific fixtures into Homestead.

Next diagnostics slice: show accepted capability details more richly in `/surfaces` (badges/filtering) if useful, but keep all presenter/table/filter logic in apps/dev and keep Homestead `surface-inspector` semantic/read-only.

Next trust-boundary slice: document how external plugin surfaces flow through registry states (`registered` -> `validated` -> `active`) and where Homestead surface activation gates differ from PluginHost execution gates.

If apps/social is introduced, start it by consuming `bootStudioRuntime(...)`, `setupStudioShell(...)`, and `createStudioPluginHandle(...)`/`createHomesteadSurfacePluginHandle(...)` rather than copying dev/me boot code.
Run `npm run clean:light --silent` only at checkpoint boundaries if generated artifacts grow meaningfully; avoid heavy cleanup while disk remains around 9GB free.
open_questions: Should `setupStudioShell(...)` remain the only shell helper, or should a later `bootStudioHost(...)` compose runtime+shell while still requiring an explicit host plugin-registration callback?

Should `createHomesteadSurfacePluginHandle(...)` enforce `ui:panel:render` for `panel` surfaces, or should enforcement remain only in the Homestead activation plan so examples can deliberately prove rejection paths? Current implementation only normalizes layer/boilerplate.

Should mounted surface capabilities be encoded in the DOM as a space-separated data attribute long-term, or should future inspector helpers correlate mounted DOM with telemetry/manifests for richer structured metadata? Current implementation is simple and test-covered.
Should a future presenter package exist for shared diagnostic UI, or should `/surfaces` stay apps/dev-only until another host repeats the pattern?
What is the long-term trust boundary for executable plugins: Homestead-only surface activation gate, PluginHost activation gate, or both with different responsibilities?
key_decisions_pending: Whether to push df008f9a plus this handoff checkpoint immediately.
Whether the next lane should be an executable stream-surface plugin example or further `/surfaces` diagnostics UX.
How far to generalize Studio local plugin handles before a real social/me plugin registration flow exercises them.
Whether accepted capability metadata should become part of a more formal telemetry schema in Tractor/manifest docs.
